steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that people spend more time viewing art in a museum than online. To support this, a choice must show a concrete time difference between those two viewing contexts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Most people spend about three minutes viewing *Dos Mujeres (Salvadora y Herminia)* at the museum and only about one minute viewing it online.": Directly compares viewing time in both settings — museum time is longer, which is exactly what the claim needs. ✓
  • "*Dos Mujeres (Salvadora y Herminia)* isn't the only work of art by Frida Kahlo that has been put online.": Says nothing about how long people look at art. ✗
  • "Most people who go to museums in Boston are visiting from other places.": Visitor origin is irrelevant to viewing duration. ✗
  • "More than one museum has works of art by Frida Kahlo in its full collection.": How large a collection is tells us nothing about time spent viewing. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
